items.reason: All 20 decisions DEC-001..DEC-020 in `docs/decisions/standards-overlap-decisions.md` carry id, question, options_considered, choice, consequences, and projects_to. `decisions-needed.md` enumerates the required areas (cross-spec overlaps + DEC-011..DEC-020 platform-shape items) and each maps to an existing decision; consequences sections name concrete artifacts (e.g., DEC-016 'Removes direct edits to dictionary/oneroster-core.v1.json...'). projects_to lists are non-empty and point at real files.

`docs/decisions/decisions-pending.md` lists PEND-001 (target 2026-05-15) and PEND-005 (target 2026-05-03) — both have owner (Andy), target_date, blocker, and rationale. Research date is 2026-04-26, so no target dates are past. Each pending entry references a tracking decision (DEC-012, DEC-015) so they are not silent TBDs.

Each decision's consequences section starts with 'Removes the need for...' or 'Eliminates...' and names a concrete artifact, e.g., DEC-007 'Removes duplicate source-ID columns from every public table; public.source_identifiers owns...' and DEC-016 'Removes direct edits to dictionary/oneroster-core.v1.json...'. Decisions that preserve coexisting surfaces (DEC-001 person+agent, DEC-002 course+class, DEC-005 line_item+result+score) include explicit 'Why not collapse' justifications against the simpler alternative.

Each decision lists three credible options bracketing the design space, e.g., DEC-005 considers AGS-only canonical / source-native peers / current state-with-inputs (chosen); DEC-016 considers shared-only-on-demand / per-spec canonical / shared-canonical-with-committed-projections; DEC-014 considers delete-all-mirrors / treat-as-canonical-contract / keep-as-doc-fixtures. The aggressive bookends (e.g., 'Use AGS as the only canonical gradebook model', 'Delete all static mirrors') reflect real production choices, not strawmen.

`scripts/generate_spec_dictionaries.py` reads `data/data-dictionary.seed.json` and writes the five `dictionary/*.v1.json` projections; `--check` mode is wired into `VERIFY.md` and into `scripts/check_dictionary_artifacts.py` (`check_spec_dictionary_projection_drift`). The seed's `projection_summary` reports 575 fields across 5 specs (not a stub), and DEC-016 declares hand-editing per-spec dictionaries a build error.

The seed's `canonical_objects[].fields[].projections` shows the canonical email field projecting to `dictionary/oneroster-core.v1.json#person.email`, `dictionary/caliper-core.v1.json#caliper_actor.email`, `dictionary/qti-core.v1.json#qti_candidate.email`, and `dictionary/integration-governance-core.v1.json#lti_membership.email` — all four overlapping email fields point at one canonical entry. The generator copies `canonical_field_id` into per-spec fields and the QTI candidate exception is documented as `spec_only` with reason citing DEC-012/PEND-001.

`canonical.identity.person` is a first-class object in `data/data-dictionary.seed.json#canonical_objects` with its own fields (id, display_name, email, source_subject_id, identity_kind), allowed values, and explicit projections for OneRoster `person`, Caliper `caliper_actor`, QTI `qti_candidate`, LTI `lti_membership`, and the LTI launch `subject_id`. The QTI projection is documented as a `spec_only` exception until PEND-001 ships the runtime, satisfying the substance check.

Each per-spec dictionary file (`dictionary/caliper-core.v1.json`, `case-core.v1.json`, `oneroster-core.v1.json`, `integration-governance-core.v1.json`, `qti-core.v1.json`) carries `shared_enums[]` with `canonical_values`, bidirectional `crosswalk` and `reverse_crosswalk` arrays. Cross-spec mappings like `grading_progress` show OneRoster `fullyGraded` and LTI `FullyGraded` both mapping to canonical `fully_graded`. `scripts/check_dictionary_artifacts.py.check_shared_enum` enforces that every enum field references a shared enum and every value is in the crosswalk.

DEC-019 enumerates the closed list (public, operational, directory, education_record, behavioral, sensitive, credential, privacy_governance, system) and `scripts/generate_spec_dictionaries.py.validate_privacy_class` rejects any value in `INVALID_PRIVACY_CLASSES = {depends_on_contents, depends_on_entity}` or starting with `depends_on_`. Validation runs against object and field privacy_class entries, including in the canonical object registry, so per-spec divergence is impossible without a seed change.

The seed's `migration_generation` block declares runtime_object_keys, table_order, and 13 relationship_keys for the OneRoster + Caliper runtime tables. `scripts/generate_supabase_migrations.py` reads `objects[].relationships` and emits `supabase/migrations/0001_oneroster_core_demo.sql`, with `--check` wired into `VERIFY.md` and into `scripts/check_dictionary_artifacts.py.check_supabase_migration_drift`. The validator enforces that each relationship has source_field, target_field, cardinality, ownership, delete_behavior, required, and decision_id citing DEC-020.

Every field in the per-spec dictionaries carries a `decision_id` (e.g., `dictionary/oneroster-core.v1.json` shows `DEC-001-person-agent-subject` on person fields, `DEC-018-global-enum-crosswalks` on shared enum canonical values). `scripts/check_dictionary_artifacts.py.check_dictionary_shape` cross-references each decision_id against the parsed decision-trace block in `docs/decisions/standards-overlap-decisions.md` and fails on broken links or fields missing from `produces_fields`.

Each spec has a generator (`scripts/generate_oneroster_core.py`, `generate_qti_core.py`, `generate_case_core.py`, `generate_caliper_core.py`, `generate_integration_governance_core.py`) that emits SQL comments, OpenAPI JSON, Markdown, and HTML from the per-spec dictionary; `scripts/check_dictionary_artifacts.py.check_artifacts` enforces that descriptions, decision_ids, sourceStandard, and enum values all match the dictionary. The buildability guide and other prose pages link to dictionary anchors (e.g., `oneroster-core-dictionary.html#person.email`), and `site/api/site-link-check.json` reports zero broken links.

`docs/lead-spec-accounting.md` includes a 'Decision Citation Map' explaining each Lead area's posture in plain language with the controlling decision_id; `docs/build-an-edtech-app.md` cites DEC-010 inline ('runtime tenant_id column is filled from the JWT claim and checked by row-level security, per DEC-010'); the decision register itself is rendered as `site/docs/decisions-standards-overlap-decisions.html` with anchors per decision (verified by `check_decision_reachability`).

`docs/build-an-edtech-app.md` walks through all five required steps with copy-pasteable curl: (1) creates the school org and 10 students via POST /rest/v1/organizations and /rest/v1/people, (2) creates academic_session/course/class/enrollments, (3) posts a numeric grade via line_items + results, (4) PATCHes `line_items.case_target_uri` with the CASE standard URI, (5) reads `class_activity_feed` for the Caliper-style stream. Each step has a 'Dictionary fields and values used' table linking every field and enum value to its dictionary anchor (e.g., `oneroster-core-dictionary.html#enum.role_family.teacher`).

`site/api/site-link-check.json` reports `status: pass`, `errors: 0`, 223 local links checked, 634 dictionary entries validated, 20 decisions reachable, 12 API endpoints checked, and all 36 HTML pages reachable from `site/index.html` within 3 clicks. The buildability guide is reachable in 1 click and the link checker (`scripts/check_site_links.py`) enforces dictionary object/field anchors plus decision anchors.

Simulating a layperson reading only `docs/`: the 'Choose A Runtime' section provides a self-hosted path (Supabase project + `psql -f supabase/migrations/0001_oneroster_core_demo.sql`) so a developer without platform admin access can complete the scenario; Step 0 produces `PLATFORM_ACCESS_TOKEN` via Auth admin + password grant; Steps 1-5 are executable curl against PostgREST with all field names linked to the dictionary and all enum values linked to the global crosswalk (e.g., `enum.organization_type.school`, `enum.role_family.teacher`, `enum.gradebook_category.assignment`, `enum.event_type.GradeEvent`). `tenant_id` is documented as JWT-derived per DEC-010, the CASE step PATCHes a documented column (`line_items.case_target_uri`), and the Caliper feed reads from `class_activity_feed` view defined by the migration generator. No spec conversions, ambiguous enums, or missing endpoints surfaced.

`scripts/evaluate_platform.py.ensure_traced_causes` enforces a non-empty `traced_cause` on every non-pass verdict and falls back to `DEFAULT_TRACED_CAUSES` keyed by rubric id; `normalize_buildability_gaps` injects per-gap traced causes (extracted from reason or fallback to the buildability item's traced cause). The prompt template demands 'Name the lowest-layer artifact that must change and the required change.' This evaluator implementation also satisfies its own item.

`VERIFY.md` ends with `python3 scripts/evaluate_platform.py --output site/api/platform-evaluation.json`, so the evaluator runs at the end of every loop iteration. The script writes per-item `traced_cause`, a `buildability_gaps` array (with traced causes), `counts`, and a `done` flag; `apply_prereq_blocks` flips items to `blocked` with named prerequisites instead of `fail` when infrastructure is missing.

`scripts/codex_loop.py.read_overrides` reads `docs/loop-overrides.md` at the start of every iteration; `overrides_paused` halts before launching Codex when a bare `PAUSE` token sits on its own line under `## Pause` (HTML-comment template lines are stripped); `format_overrides_for_prompt` injects the cleaned text under a 'Human overrides from `docs/loop-overrides.md` (AUTHORITATIVE — these supersede rubric defaults...)' section in the prompt. The PAUSE check runs before the next Codex invocation, not after.

`scripts/codex_loop.py` re-runs the evaluator after each iteration (`run_evaluator(... label='after')`) and only stops when `after_eval.get('done')` is true. When Codex's stdout contains 'LOOP_STATUS: COMPLETE' but the verifier disagrees, the driver logs 'Codex reported COMPLETE but evaluator says work remains. Continuing.' and proceeds to the next iteration, so termination is verifier-driven, not narrative-driven. Stall detection uses rubric counts (`rubric_progressed`), not numeric-threshold gating.

`maybe_publish` in `scripts/codex_loop.py` runs `git fetch origin main` then `git pull --rebase origin main` before `git push origin main`; on rebase failure it collects conflicting paths via `git diff --name-only --diff-filter=U`, calls `git rebase --abort`, and returns a `DIVERGENCE: ...` string that the main loop catches with `if publish_result.startswith('DIVERGENCE:'): return 1`, halting before the next iteration. PROGRESS.md records the publish_result string for every iteration via `append_machine_progress`. The driver does not retry on `rejected (fetch first)` because rebase happens before push.

projections.note: Structural per-decision projection index generated from `projects_to`; semantic contradictions are graded by the projections_match_reality rubric item.

decisions.title: DEC-001 Person, User, Actor, Profile, and Subject
decisions.choice: The platform has a canonical `person` for known school people and a broader `agent` concept for non-person actors. Credential subjects and Caliper actors resolve to `person` only when policy and identifiers allow it; otherwise they remain linked external agents.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.
Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-002 Class, Course, Context, Group, Course Section, and Organization
decisions.choice: The platform separates `course` from `class`. `course` is the catalog/template. `class` is the scheduled teaching section. LTI contexts and Caliper groups can map to a class, course, or other learning context. Common Cartridge organizations are content outlines, not school organizations or scheduled classes.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-003 Roles
decisions.choice: Store source roles exactly, then map them to platform role families: `learner`, `educator`, `administrator`, `guardian`, `mentor`, `staff`, `tool`, `issuer`, and `unknown`.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.
Referenced artifact contains the projection's primary token.

decisions.title: DEC-004 Enrollment and Membership
decisions.choice: `enrollment` is the canonical roster participation record. `membership` is a contextual service/event view that may be derived from enrollment or captured from an external system.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-005 Results, Scores, Outcomes, and Grade Events
decisions.choice: The platform uses `line_item` as the gradebook target and `result` as the learner's current gradebook state. LTI AGS Score is an incoming write/update message. LTI AGS Result and OneRoster Result map to current result state. QTI outcomes are assessment-runtime variables projected into results only when a delivery or scoring workflow declares that mapping. Caliper GradeEvent is event history, not the gradebook source of truth.

decisions.title: DEC-006 Standards Alignment
decisions.choice: CASE is the canonical standards graph. Other standards store alignment as source metadata and resolve known targets to CASE item identifiers or URIs.

decisions.title: DEC-007 Identifiers
decisions.choice: Internal records have platform IDs. Standard-shaped endpoints expose the standard-native ID as the primary contract when required. Every external ID is stored in a source identifier crosswalk with source system, identifier type, and object relationship.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-008 Time, School Sessions, and Event Timestamps
decisions.choice: Separate calendar periods, event timestamps, availability windows, and validity periods. Store timestamps in UTC with source timezone/offset when provided. Store OneRoster AcademicSession as the school calendar backbone.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-009 Content, Resources, Packages, and Launch Links
decisions.choice: The platform uses a broad `resource` concept for discoverable learning objects, with subtype-specific records for cartridge resources, QTI packages/items/tests, LTI resource links, and Caliper event entities.

decisions.title: DEC-010 Tenant-Owned Data and Shared Reference Data
decisions.choice: Tenant-owned operational records carry tenant boundaries and row-level policy. Public CASE frameworks, public standards metadata, public certification fixtures, and optionally public item banks live in shared reference namespaces. Tenants adopt, pin, override, or privately extend shared reference data through explicit records.

projections.detail: Referenced artifact contains the projection's primary token.
Referenced artifact contains the projection's primary token.

decisions.title: DEC-011 Privacy Surfaces
decisions.choice: Generated docs may describe every field and privacy class because they are schema documentation, not records. Live PostgREST may expose tenant-owned `public`, `operational`, `directory`, and `education_record` records only through tenant RLS and scope policy. Edge Functions may expose `directory`, `education_record`, `behavioral`, or sensitive operational fields only through caller JWT, purpose, scope, tenant RLS, and audit logging when the read is sensitive. Static `site/api/*.json` mirrors may contain only synthetic demo records from the seeded `.test` tenant and may include `public`, `operational`, and `directory` fields such as demo email; they may not contain real tenant data, `education_record`, `behavioral`, `sensitive_pii`, or secret/system values.

decisions.title: DEC-012 Runtime Coverage Per Spec
decisions.choice: OneRoster core is runtime-backed now through local SQLite, hosted Supabase PostgREST, RLS, and live tests. The teaching-app runtime also backs the narrow CASE alignment path needed by gradebook line items (`line_items.case_target_uri`) and the narrow Caliper class-feed path needed by the buildability guide (`caliper_events` plus `class_activity_feed`). QTI and full CASE import/search remain doc-only/generated dictionary projections until runtime slices are built. Caliper, LTI, Security Framework, and Data Privacy have partial runtime backing through authenticated table, view, Edge Function receipt, or governance paths, but not full standard conformance flows. Full runtime for QTI import, CASE search, full Sensor API ingestion/projection, LTI Advantage services, production OAuth, and privacy workflows remains future work with target dates in the decision/pending work queue.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.
Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-013 Audit Response Truth
decisions.choice: Audited Edge Functions must use truthful responses when they include an `audit` block. If the function cannot read the written `audit_log` rows back under the caller's JWT and request identifier, it must omit the audit block or return an explicit error; it must not hard-code `logged` counts or field lists.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.
Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-014 Static Mirror Policy
decisions.choice: Static mirrors are allowed as documentation fixtures for GitHub Pages and offline inspection. They are not the canonical runtime contract; live Supabase REST and Edge Functions are the runtime contract. Mirrors must be generated from the same synthetic demo seed as the local demo, must carry source metadata, and may only include privacy classes allowed by `DEC-011-privacy-surfaces`.

decisions.title: DEC-015 Service-Role Policy
decisions.choice: Request-scoped runtime code and Edge Functions must pass the caller's `Authorization` bearer token to Supabase so RLS applies. Service-role access is allowed only for allowlisted admin or test fixture operations in `docs/admin-operations.md`, with a named operation ID, caller, why-user-JWT-insufficient reason, guardrails, and recent review date. Tests may use service role to create temporary Auth users, but not to read tenant data or satisfy the assertion under test.

decisions.title: DEC-016 Dictionary Generation Direction
decisions.choice: `data/data-dictionary.seed.json` is upstream. `dictionary/*.v1.json` files are generated projections; hand-editing a per-spec projection is a build error once the generator lands.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.
Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-017 Canonical Field Reconciliation
decisions.choice: Every overlapping spec field carries `canonical_field_id` pointing at the shared dictionary. Spec-native names remain for conformance; cross-spec app code uses the canonical field identity.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-018 Global Enum Crosswalks
decisions.choice: Allowed values are global dictionary objects. Each enum field references `shared_enum_id`; the shared enum maps every spec-native value to and from a canonical value.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-019 Closed Privacy Classes
decisions.choice: Closed keys are `public`, `operational`, `directory`, `education_record`, `behavioral`, `sensitive`, `credential`, `privacy_governance`, and `system`. `depends_on_entity` and `depends_on_contents` are invalid.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-020 Relational Graph Migrations
decisions.choice: Shared dictionary objects own source field, target object, target field, cardinality, ownership, and delete behavior. Supabase migrations are generated from that graph.
